props.title: Frequently Asked Questions about Air Filtration Systems in {city}

faqs.question: What does an air filtration system do?
faqs.answer: An air filtration system removes airborne contaminants like dust, pollen, pet dander, mold spores, and bacteria from your indoor air. It works alongside your HVAC system to continuously clean the air circulating through your home.

faqs.question: How often should air filters be replaced?
faqs.answer: Standard filters should be replaced every 1-3 months depending on usage, pets, and allergies. HEPA and media filters can last 6-12 months. We'll recommend the right schedule based on your system and household needs.

faqs.question: Can an air filtration system help with allergies?
faqs.answer: Yes. High-quality air filtration systems with HEPA or equivalent filters can remove up to 99.97% of airborne particles, significantly reducing allergy and asthma triggers in your home.
